content.full_text:
•

•

FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

January 29, 2009

Rodney J. Blonien
Blonien & Associates, Inc.
1121 L Street, Suite 507
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-09-008

Dear Mr. Blonien:

This letter confirms the receipt of your January 8, 2009, request for advice
regarding a former member of the California Horse Racing Board and the revolving door
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B)
and (c)(4)(C), unless the former board member has authorized you as his or her
representative, the Fair Political Practices Commission (the "Commission") cannot
address your questions or provide analysis. I have enclosed a copy of Regulation 18329
for your review.²

In addition, the Commission does not act as the finder of fact when it renders
advice. (In re Oglesby (9175) 1 FPPC Ops. 71. It is the Commission's policy not to
answer purely hypothetical questions, or questions where the facts presented are
insufficient or too vague to render specific advice. (Regulation 18329.) For general
information regarding the Act's revolving door provisions (Sections 87400-87407), you
may refer to the Commission's fact sheet entitled "Leaving Your State Job? Post-

___________________________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All
statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California
Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of
Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

² Note that other bodies of law outside the purview of the Act may also apply to the facts you
have presented. As the Commission may not issue advice outside the confines of the Act, we offer no
opinion on the application, if any, of other conflict-of-interest laws, Government Code Section 1090, or the
post-government employment restrictions of Public Contract Code Section 10411. You may wish to
consult the Attorney General's office regarding these provisions.

File No. W-09-008
Page No. 2

Employment Restrictions May Affect You" on the Commission's website,
www.fppc.ca.gov/index.html?id=33.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Brian G. Lau
Counsel, Legal Division

BGL:jgl

Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: The requester sought advice regarding whether a former California Horse Racing Board member was subject to the Political Reform Act's revolving door provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requester was not authorized as the former board member's representative, and the Commission does not address third-party inquiries without proper authorization under Regulation 18329(b)(5)(B) and (c)(4)(C).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter (file number W-09-008 indicates withdrawal/declination). No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The letter explains the Commission cannot address the inquiry without proper authorization from the interested party. References to other applicable laws and suggestion to consult Attorney General's office are noted.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The requester sought advice regarding whether a former California Horse Racing Board member was subject to the Political Reform Act's revolving door provisions.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC declined to provide advice because the requester was not authorized as the former board member's representative, and the Commission does not address third-party inquiries without proper authorization under Regulation 18329(b)(5)(B) and (c)(4)(C).

embedding.summary: The FPPC declined to provide advice on a third party's inquiry regarding revolving door restrictions applicable to a former California Horse Racing Board member, citing lack of authorization and the Commission's policy against rendering advice on behalf of unauthorized representatives.
